title: Kindergarten Fee Assistance Scheme (KiFAS) and KiFAS Startup Grant (SUG)
text:
Kindergarten Fee Assistance Scheme (KiFAS) and KiFAS Startup Grant (SUG) - SupportGoWhere
Kindergarten Fee Assistance Scheme (KiFAS) and KiFAS Startup Grant (SUG)
Share link
Provides subsidies for kindergarten programmes to ensure affordability for low and middle-income Singapore Citizen families, along with an annual KiFAS Start-Up Grant (SUG) to assist low-income families with initial costs.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Subsidies range between $17 and $161 per month
Eligible families will receive an additional yearly SUG (capped at $240 per year) to cover initial costs
Apply at your child's kindergarten
Collapse all sections
About this support

Subsidies range between $17 and $161 per month

Subsidy amount depends on monthly household income, and will be directly disbursed to the child's kindergarten

Eligible applicants will receive a yearly KiFAS SUG capped at $240 per year to help cover initial costs such as registration fee, deposit, school uniform, insurance, and educational material fees, in addition to the basic subsidies

Who is this for?

Child must be a Singapore Citizen

Enrolled in an Anchor Operator or Ministry of Education Kindergarten

Monthly household income of up to $12,000, or monthly household income per capita of up to $3,000 for larger families with 5 or more family members in the same household

From 1 January 2027, monthly household income thresholds will be raised to $15,000, or $3,400 per capita

Yearly SUG:

Applicants with monthly household income of up to $1,900, or monthly household income per capita of up to $650 for larger families with 5 or more family members in the same household, can further apply for SUG

Each application is assessed on a case-by-case basis

How to apply?

Refer to Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) website on steps on how to apply for subsidies

Where can I find help?

View more details

ECDA hotline: 6735 9213

Submit an enquiry

ECDA office
51 Cuppage Road
#08-01
Singapore 229469

Scheme last updated 19 May 2026
